22022421680.description:
Validation Scope

Intent Verification Strategy * PMX HW Requirements N/A SW Requirements  AcodeFW, Pcode, Pstate driver, Cstate Driver, DMR SVOS, DMR python SV repository,PAtch23 and pythonSV installed.

HAS Ref — PMX CPU/Core Power × Traffic Interaction: PMX cross-product with active CPU traffic validates that PM state transitions (C-states, P-states, DVFS) occur correctly under load. Key: core power draw with PMX-sourced traffic must not exceed per-core power limits; frequency and voltage transitions complete without glitch; traffic throughput degrades gracefully under PM throttling.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
FeaturePM PMX Cross Product
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Configure PM features for cross-product interaction. Verify baseline state of each feature.All features enabled; baseline register state matches expected defaults.
2Run the cross-product scenario: exercise both features concurrently under workload. Run the automation script.Both features operate correctly under combined load; no unexpected interaction, MCA, or hang.
3Collect PM registers, RAPL counters, and NLOG after test.All values within spec; system stable; NLOG error-free.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PMX feature state registers consistent before and after PM transitions.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.
22022421680.title: PM PMX Cross Product

22022421695.description:
Validation Scope

Validate pmx Security x PC6 on NWP silicon/emulation.

HAS Ref — PMX Security × PC6 Interaction: Security flows (TDX, SGX, TME, MKTME) must not interfere with PC6 entry/exit. Key check: PC6 entry with active security workload — PCode must ensure security context save/restore is complete before PC6 collapse and after PC6 exit. MCA on premature PC6 entry during security flow is a validation failure. Registers: PC6_RESIDENCY_MSR, SECURITY_STATE_REG, PKG_C_STATE_LIMIT.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Featurepmx Security x PC6
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Start a security workload (TDX/SGX/MKTME) and confirm it is active.Security workload running; security state registers non-zero.
2Initiate system entry into PC6 while security workload is active.PC6 entry deferred until security context is saved; no MCA on entry.
3Verify PC6 exit restores security context correctly.Security state restored after PC6 exit; workload resumes without error.
4Run the automation script.Automation PASS; PMX Security × PC6 interaction verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PMX feature state registers consistent before and after PM transitions.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • cid:6b8b6248-dc82-45db-8077-9e92cadc5e27
22022421695.title: pmx Security x PC6

22022421698.description:
Validation Scope

Verify SGX and TDX are enabled: msr -r -o 1401     --> in that we need to verify bit 10 and 11 are set (TDX) cpuid 0x12  -->check for out_eax --> check bit 0 and 1 should be set (SGX) PMX command to run RACL: runPmx. Feature tag: RACL.

HAS Ref — PMX Security × RACL Interaction: RACL (Resource Access Control Layer) enforces access policies during PM state transitions. Cross-product validation: RACL access checks must not block or delay C-state entry/exit; RACL violations during PM transitions must be correctly signalled without causing system hang or silent data corruption. Registers: RACL_POLICY_REG, RACL_STATUS, PM state MSRs.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Featurepmx Security x RACL
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Start a security workload (TDX/SGX/MKTME) and confirm it is active.Security workload running; security state registers non-zero.
2Initiate system entry into PC6 while security workload is active.PC6 entry deferred until security context is saved; no MCA on entry.
3Verify PC6 exit restores security context correctly.Security state restored after PC6 exit; workload resumes without error.
4Run the automation script.Automation PASS; PMX Security × PC6 interaction verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PMX feature state registers consistent before and after PM transitions.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.
22022421698.title: pmx Security x RACL

22022421701.description:
Validation Scope

This test's purpose is to use stress several PM IPs such as: -Cstates -HWP -DVFS Steps  1 -  Boot the system to S0. Feature tag: Solar cross products.

HAS Ref — Solar Cross-Product: C-state × DVFS: Solar (IMH subsystem) cross-product tests exercise C-state transitions concurrently with DVFS (Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling). Covered: C0→C6 entry/exit while frequency steps are in progress; voltage droop on C-state exit combined with frequency ramp; DVFS completion before deep C-state collapse. Validation: no MCA, hang, or frequency overshoot; DVFS ack received before C-state entry; exit latency within HAS-specified bound.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[Solar Cross-Product] Cross_Products-Allcross-cstate_hwp_p0_pn_dvfs_random
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation command/usr/bin/solar/solar.sh /cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/pm/Solar/Cross Products/cstate_hwp_p0_pn_dvfs_random.xml /logpath .
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Apply a workload inducing platform DVFS transitions. Monitor frequency and voltage.DVFS transitions trigger; frequency and voltage change as expected; no overshoot or undershoot beyond HAS tolerance.
2Start Sideband Harasser (if cross-product) or secondary PM load concurrently.DVFS transitions continue correctly under combined load; no sideband timeout or PM error.
3Verify DVFS ack/completion signalling. Check PERF_STATUS reflects correct operating point.PERF_STATUS matches requested operating point within one DVFS cycle.
4Run /usr/bin/solar/solar.sh /cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/pm/Solar/Cross Products/cstate_hwp_p0_pn_dvfs_random.xml /logpath ..Automation PASS; platform DVFS cross-product verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PERF_STATUS matches requested operating point after DVFS; no stuck frequency.
  • Solar PM state telemetry consistent; no out-of-order state transition.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

HAS Ref — Solar Cross-Product: P-state × DVFS: Solar P-state transitions tested concurrently with DVFS. Covered: OS-requested P-state change (via CPPC/HWP) during active DVFS sequence; simultaneous P-state requests from multiple cores; P-state and voltage convergence under combined traffic. Validation: P-state request honoured within HAS latency; voltage and frequency settle to correct operating point; no spurious PROCHOT or power violation during transition.

Validation: no MCA, hang, or frequency overshoot; DVFS ack received before C-state entry; exit latency within HAS-specified bound.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[Solar Cross-Product] Cross_Products-Cstate_DVFS-cstate_dvfs_random_exercise
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation command/usr/bin/solar/solar.sh /cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/Cstate_DVFS/Cross_Products/cstate_dvfs_random_exercise.xml /logpath .
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Apply a workload inducing platform DVFS transitions. Monitor frequency and voltage.DVFS transitions trigger; frequency and voltage change as expected; no overshoot or undershoot beyond HAS tolerance.
2Start Sideband Harasser (if cross-product) or secondary PM load concurrently.DVFS transitions continue correctly under combined load; no sideband timeout or PM error.
3Verify DVFS ack/completion signalling. Check PERF_STATUS reflects correct operating point.PERF_STATUS matches requested operating point within one DVFS cycle.
4Run /usr/bin/solar/solar.sh /cfg /usr/local/python/diamondrapids/pm/Solar/Cstate_DVFS/Cross_Products/cstate_dvfs_random_exercise.xml /logpath ..Automation PASS; platform DVFS cross-product verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • PERF_STATUS matches requested operating point after DVFS; no stuck frequency.
  • Solar PM state telemetry consistent; no out-of-order state transition.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

16030715653.description:
Validation Scope

This TC verifies that TSOD-driven CLTT is disabled in NWP.

HAS Ref — ZBB Negative Checks (Zero-Bug-Bounce): ZBB negative validation exercises error paths to confirm PCode gracefully handles out-of-spec inputs without MCA or hang. Covered domains: DRAM RAPL negative (invalid PL values), Memory PM negative (bad DIMM state requests), PKGC negative (forced entry with blocking conditions), Platform RAPL/PSYS negative (out-of-range power limits), SST-PP/CP/BF negative (invalid profile or bucket selects). Expected: PCode clamps or rejects invalid values; NLOG records the event; no fatal MCA or system hang.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[PSS]Memory PM ZBB Negative Checks
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Configure Memory PM feature with an out-of-spec / invalid parameter (e.g., power limit below minimum, invalid profile index).System accepts input; PCode clamps or rejects value gracefully; no immediate MCA or hang.
2Verify PCode NLOG records the invalid-parameter event with appropriate error code or warning.NLOG entry present; error code matches expected Memory PM rejection path.
3Verify system continues normal operation after invalid parameter attempt: PM state transitions, C-states, and power limiting still functional.System stable; normal PM operations unaffected after negative-test stimulus.
4Run the automation script.Automation PASS; Memory PM ZBB negative checks verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • Key registers readable with expected values: sv.socket0.imh0.memss.mcs.mcscheds_common.dimm_temp_ev_ctrl, sv.socket0.imh0.memss.mc1.mcscheds_common.dimm_tsod_temp
  • NLOG records invalid-parameter event with expected error code.
  • System remains stable and PM-functional after each negative test.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • https://docs.intel.com/documents/custom-xeon/newport-docs/has/overview/nwp_has.html#nwp-memory-feature-list-delta
  • https://docs.intel.com/documents/custom-xeon/newport-docs/platform/pas/NwpPAS.html#on-dram-thermal-sensor
  • https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/mc/HAS/Gen6/Releases/LPDDR6/lpddr6.html#Features_Top
16030715653.title: [PSS]Memory PM ZBB Negative Checks

22022423114.description:
Validation Scope

Intent Verification Strategy * How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e. Feature tag: SB Harasser.

HAS Ref — Sideband Harasser Traffic: The Sideband (SB) Harasser generates continuous GPSB/PMSB traffic to stress the PM sideband fabric while other PM features run concurrently. Covered cross-products: CBB DVFS × SB harasser, DRAM RAPL × SB harasser, PEGA/Solar C-states × SB harasser, Solar/iMH P-states × SB harasser. Validation: no sideband timeout, no PM register access failure, no MCA from fabric contention; NLOG error-free.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
FeatureCBB DVFS x SB Harasser traffic
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation commandecho manual_execution
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Start the Sideband Harasser to generate continuous GPSB/PMSB traffic.Harasser running; sideband traffic visible; no initial error.
2Simultaneously exercise the CBB DVFS feature under normal workload.CBB DVFS transitions proceed normally; no PM register access failure.
3Monitor for sideband timeouts, PM register access errors, and NLOG errors throughout the concurrent run (minimum 60 s).Zero sideband timeouts; zero PM access errors; NLOG error-free for duration.
4Stop harasser. Verify system PM state is consistent and feature still functional.PM state stable after harasser stop; no latent error; CBB DVFS feature operates correctly.
5Run echo manual_execution.Automation PASS; CBB DVFS × SB Harasser cross-product verified.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • Zero sideband (GPSB/PMSB) timeouts for the duration of harasser run.
  • All PM register accesses succeed; no NAK or unreachable endpoint.
  • PERF_STATUS matches requested operating point after DVFS; no stuck frequency.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

22022423121.description:
Validation Scope

Intent Verification Strategy * How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e. Feature tag: SB Harasser.

HAS Ref — GPSB/PMSB Endpoint Sweep: Systematic access to all registered GPSB (General Purpose Sideband) and PMSB (Power Management Sideband) endpoints on CBB and iMH. Validation: each endpoint responds to read/write transactions; no timeout, no unreachable endpoint; register values within defined range. Sweep covers all IP instances: PUnit, FIVR controllers, thermal sensors, RAPL counters, PM configuration registers.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
FeatureCBB GPSB end point sweep
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation commandecho manual_execution
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Enumerate all CBB GPSB endpoints from the topology. Record expected count and address range.All expected GPSB endpoints discoverable; count matches topology spec.
2Issue read transactions to each GPSB endpoint in sequence. Record response and data.Each endpoint responds without timeout; returned data within defined register range.
3Issue write-then-read-back to writable GPSB registers. Verify data integrity.Write accepted; read-back matches written value; no silent corruption.
4Run echo manual_execution.Automation PASS; CBB GPSB endpoint sweep complete.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • Zero sideband (GPSB/PMSB) timeouts for the duration of harasser run.
  • All PM register accesses succeed; no NAK or unreachable endpoint.
  • All enumerated endpoints respond; no timeout or missing endpoint.
  • Read-back of written registers matches; no silent data corruption.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

22022423123.description:
Validation Scope

Intent Verification Strategy * How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e. Feature tag: SB Harasser.

HAS Ref — GPSB/PMSB Endpoint Sweep: Systematic access to all registered GPSB (General Purpose Sideband) and PMSB (Power Management Sideband) endpoints on CBB and iMH. Validation: each endpoint responds to read/write transactions; no timeout, no unreachable endpoint; register values within defined range. Sweep covers all IP instances: PUnit, FIVR controllers, thermal sensors, RAPL counters, PM configuration registers.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
FeatureiMH GPSB end point sweep
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation commandecho manual_execution
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Enumerate all iMH GPSB endpoints from the topology. Record expected count and address range.All expected GPSB endpoints discoverable; count matches topology spec.
2Issue read transactions to each GPSB endpoint in sequence. Record response and data.Each endpoint responds without timeout; returned data within defined register range.
3Issue write-then-read-back to writable GPSB registers. Verify data integrity.Write accepted; read-back matches written value; no silent corruption.
4Run echo manual_execution.Automation PASS; iMH GPSB endpoint sweep complete.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • Zero sideband (GPSB/PMSB) timeouts for the duration of harasser run.
  • All PM register accesses succeed; no NAK or unreachable endpoint.
  • All enumerated endpoints respond; no timeout or missing endpoint.
  • Read-back of written registers matches; no silent data corruption.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

ItemRequirement
FeatureiMH PMSB end point sweep
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Automation commandecho manual_execution
Category / noteActive PM
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Enumerate all iMH PMSB endpoints. Record expected count.All expected PMSB endpoints discoverable; no missing entry.
2Issue PM read command to each PMSB endpoint. Record response.Each endpoint responds within timeout; data within spec; no NAK or error response.
3Issue write to configurable PMSB registers; read back and verify.Read-back matches write; no bus error or timeout.
4Run echo manual_execution.Automation PASS; iMH PMSB endpoint sweep complete.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • Zero sideband (GPSB/PMSB) timeouts for the duration of harasser run.
  • All PM register accesses succeed; no NAK or unreachable endpoint.
  • All enumerated endpoints respond; no timeout or missing endpoint.
  • Read-back of written registers matches; no silent data corruption.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.
